title: 10 best herbal teas for sore throat

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme tea's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help combat bacterial infections that can cause sore throats by inhibiting their growth and replication.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in thyme tea have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling and pain in the throat and promoting healing of mucous membranes.

Expectorant properties: Thyme tea's expectorant compounds help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion and discomfort associated with sore throats.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, teas helps with sore throat because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in thyme, such as thymol, have been shown to reduce swelling and combat infections in the throat. Thyme teas also contain mucilages, which provide soothing relief to the irritated tissues, allowing for faster healing and reducing discomfort. Additionally, thyme's antiseptic properties help to kill bacteria and viruses that cause sore throats, making it an effective natural remedy.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol is a monoterpenic phenol that acts as an antibacterial and antifungal agent, helping to reduce the growth of pathogens that can cause sore throats.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol is a phenolic monoterpene with antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections and reduce inflammation in the throat, alleviating sore throat symptoms.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid is a polyphenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the throat, as well as combat oxidative stress caused by infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are commonly used for sore throat teas due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are often used to make soothing teas for sore throats, as they contain antitussive and expectorant properties.
Stems: Thyme stems are sometimes used in teas for sore throats, as they contain compounds that may help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried thymus vulgaris leaves and 1 teaspoon of honey for sweetening.
Heat 1 cup of boiling water for 1-2 minutes to prepare the brewing liquid.
Steep the thymus vulgaris leaves in the hot water for 5-7 minutes to release flavors.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve to remove the plant material.
Add the honey and stir well to combine with the herbal tea.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as menthol and menthone, help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with a sore throat, providing relief from pain and discomfort.

Antibacterial properties: The antimicrobial compounds in peppermint tea, including rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, combat bacterial infections that can cause or exacerbate a sore throat, promoting healing and recovery.

Soothing mucilage: Peppermint tea's mucilages, such as mucilage from the peppermint plant itself, form a protective barrier on the mucous membranes in the throat, providing comfort and relief by reducing irritation and inflammation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, teas helps with sore throat because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The menthol content in peppermint oil helps to reduce swelling and ease pain in the throat, making it easier to swallow and speak. Additionally, peppermint's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus, promoting a faster recovery from respiratory infections. Its soothing and cooling effects also provide instant relief from a sore throat, making it a popular remedy for soothing and calming irritated throats.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol acts as a local anesthetic and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to numb the throat and reduce pain.
Rosmarinic acid (phenolic compound): Rosmarinic acid has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce swelling and soothe the inflamed tissues in the throat.
Eugenol (phenolic compound): Eugenol has antimicrobial properties, helping to combat infections and reduce inflammation in the throat, thereby alleviating sore throat symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Mentha x piperita are commonly used to make teas for sore throat due to their high concentration of menthol, which provides anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Buds: The buds of Mentha x piperita are used to make teas for sore throat because they contain a higher concentration of menthol and other oils that help to reduce inflammation and soothe the throat.

Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita are used to make teas for sore throat, although less commonly than leaves and buds, as they still contain menthol and other oils that provide anti-inflammatory and soothing effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers early in the morning for optimal flavor.
Rinse the harvested leaves and flowers in cold running water for 2 minutes to remove any dirt.
Combine the rinsed mentha x piperita leaves and flowers with 1 quart of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass pitcher.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the desired flavor and aroma are achieved.
Strain the tea into cups using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: The polyphenolic compounds present in purple coneflower tea, such as cinnamic acid and ferulic acid, help to reduce inflammation in the throat, providing relief from soreness and discomfort.

Antimicrobial: The bioactive compounds found in purple coneflower tea, including phenolic acids and flavonoids, exhibit antimicrobial properties that can help to combat infections causing sore throats, such as strep and flu viruses.

Soothing mucilage: The tea's mucilaginous properties coat the throat and mouth tissues with a protective layer of gel-like substance, providing soothing relief from irritation, scratchiness, and discomfort associated with a sore throat.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, teas helps with sore throat because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tea contains compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid, which work together to reduce swelling and ease pain in the throat. Additionally, Echinacea's immunomodulatory effects help to stimulate the body's natural defense mechanisms, allowing it to fight off underlying infections that may be causing the sore throat. This combination of properties makes Echinacea a popular remedy for soothing sore throats and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Iridoide glycosides: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with a sore throat by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Alkylamides: Alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the throat, reducing discomfort and pain caused by a sore throat.

Cichoric acid: Cichoric acid is a phenolic compound that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent the spread of infection and reduce inflammation in the throat, thereby alleviating sore throat symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties to soothe sore throats.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antimicrobial properties to combat infections that cause sore throats.
Leaves: Have a high concentration of alkylamides and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate throat pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 cups of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves when in full bloom during warm weather conditions.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves by tying them in small bundles and hanging in a warm well-ventilated area for 7-10 days.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a tea infuser or strainer.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the hot water.
Strain the tea and serve hot or cold after the steeping period has elapsed.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal licorice teas contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
Softpensing properties: Licorice root has softpensing properties that soothe and protect the mucous membranes of the throat, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by a sore throat.

Antioxidant activity: Herbal licorice teas contain antioxidants that help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to the development of respiratory infections and exacerbate symptoms of a sore throat.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, teas helps with sore throat because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The compounds present in licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce swelling and soothe the mucous membranes in the throat. Additionally, licorice teas have antimicrobial properties, which help to combat infections that can cause sore throats. The soothing and anti-inflammatory effects of licorice teas make them a popular remedy for soothing sore throats and promoting overall throat health.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps with sore throat by reducing inflammation and suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to throat irritation and pain.
Licoricidin: It exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing discomfort and pain associated with a sore throat.

Isoliquiritigenin: It possesses anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help to modulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in the throat, thereby alleviating symptoms of a sore throat.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part of Glycyrrhiza glabra to make teas for sore throat due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: The leaves are also used to make teas for sore throat, although less commonly than the roots, as they contain smaller amounts of glycyrrhizin but still have anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers can be used to make teas for sore throat, as they contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots for every 8 ounces of water.
Boil the water for 5-7 minutes to achieve a rolling boil.
Add the dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots to the boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Allow the tea to cool for 2-3 minutes before consumption.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help reduce inflammation in the throat, relieving pain and discomfort.
Expectorant properties: The expectorant properties of ginger tea help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the throat, reducing congestion and alleviating coughing fits.
Antibacterial properties: Ginger's antibacterial properties can help combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to a sore throat, such as strep throat, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, teas helps with sore throat because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation in the throat. Ginger also has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections that cause sore throats. Additionally, ginger's natural expectorant properties can help ease congestion and promote a healthy flow of mucus, further relieving sore throat symptoms. remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These phenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the throat, providing relief from sore throat.
Shogaols: These bioactive compounds have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the throat, making them effective against sore throat.

Volatile oils (including zingiberene): The essential oils present in ginger tea have antibacterial properties, which help to fight off infections that can cause sore throat, and also have anti-inflammatory properties to reduce inflammation and pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make teas for sore throat because they contain flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and protect the throat.

Flowers: They are used to make teas for sore throat because they contain volatile oils and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to relieve coughs and soothe the throat.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of dried zingiber officinale root and place it in a tea infuser or strainer.
Heat 8 oz of boiling water in a pot for 2-3 minutes to prepare the brewing water.
Steep the zingiber officinale root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the root particles.
Let the tea cool to a comfortable temperature before serving and enjoy immediately.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lemongrass tea's antibacterial compounds, such as citral and geraniol, help combat bacterial infections that can cause sore throats.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemongrass tea, including flavonoids and terpenes, reduce swelling and pain associated with sore throats, providing relief from discomfort.
Expectorant properties: Lemongrass tea's expectorant action helps loosen and clear mucus from the throat, making it easier to expel phlegm and reducing congestion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemongrass, teas helps with sore throat because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The antiseptic properties of lemongrass tea also help to combat bacterial and viral infections that often cause sore throats. remedy_constituents: Citral: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the throat, providing relief from soreness.

Rhyzomes: Rich in citral, a natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce throat inflammation and relieve congestion.
Buds: Contain essential oils with antimicrobial properties that help to combat infections causing a sore throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the cymbopogon citratus leaves and stems from mature plants in the morning when they are at their peak potency.
Wash the harvested cymbopogon citratus leaves and stems thoroughly with cold water to remove dirt and impurities.
Chop the cleaned cymbopogon citratus leaves and stems into small pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Steep one teaspoon of chopped cymbopogon citratus leaves and stems in a cup of boiling water for five minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids then add honey or lemon to taste for a refreshing herbal drink.

remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Fennel tea helps loosen and clear mucus from the throat, making it easier to cough up and reducing congestion, which can provide relief from a sore throat.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as anethole, help reduce swelling and pain in the throat, providing relief from discomfort and inflammation associated with a sore throat.
Antimicrobial properties: Fennel's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial and viral infections that cause sore throats, reducing the duration and severity of the infection.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, teas helps with sore throat because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The tea's active compounds, such as anethole and fenchone, have been shown to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause sore throats. Additionally, fennel tea's soothing and expectorant properties can help to calm the throat and loosen mucus, making it easier to swallow and relieving discomfort. remedy_constituents: Anethole: This terpene helps with sore throat by acting as a local anesthetic and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing pain and swelling in the throat.
Foeniculin: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to combat the underlying causes of sore throat, such as oxidative stress and inflammation.
Apiol: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce infection and inflammation in the throat, thereby alleviating sore throat symptoms.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Foeniculum vulgare seeds are commonly used to make teas for sore throat due to their high content of carvone, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Foeniculum vulgare leaves are used to make teas for sore throat due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the throat.
Stems: Foeniculum vulgare stems are used to make teas for sore throat due to their ability to reduce inflammation and provide relief from throat pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 sprigs of fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers in the morning to ensure optimal potency.
Rinse the harvested foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers gently with cold water to remove dirt and debris.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried foeniculum vulgare or 2-3 sprigs of fresh foeniculum vulgare in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers.
Serve the herbal foeniculum vulgare tea hot or iced immediately after straining and enjoy the medicinal benefits.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce swelling and pain in the throat, alleviating discomfort associated with sore throats.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of stinging nettle tea can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to inflammation and tissue damage in the throat, promoting healing and reducing symptoms of a sore throat.

Mucilages and mucin-rich polysaccharides: Stinging nettle tea contains mucilages (thick, protective substances) and mucin-rich polysaccharides that can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, providing relief from irritation and discomfort associated with sore throats.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, teas helps with sore throat because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves contain compounds that have been shown to reduce swelling and ease pain. The soothing effects of Urtica dioica tea can calm an irritated throat, providing relief from discomfort and allowing the body to heal. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help combat infections that may be contributing to the soreness, promoting a faster recovery. This makes Urtica dioica tea a popular natural remedy for soothing sore throats.
remedy_constituents: Isothiocyanates: These sulfur-containing compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and soothe the throat, alleviating discomfort associated with sore throats.

Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin): As a powerful antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, Quercetin helps reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the throat, making it easier to swallow and speak.
Amino acids (specifically Histidine): Histidine has been shown to have mucolytic properties, breaking down mucus and relieving congestion in the throat, which can contribute to sore throat symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part for teas due to their high concentration of salicylic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain.
Stems: The stems of Urtica dioica contain astringent properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and soothing a sore throat.
Roots: The roots of the plant are used to make teas that help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections that may be causing the sore throat.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30 grams of dried urtica dioica leaves and flowers from a trusted herbal source.
Crush the dried urtica dioica leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the powdered urtica dioica with 1 liter of boiling water in a large heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes or until the liquid reaches a temperature of 95 to 99 degrees fahrenheit.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container to remove the solids.

remedy_properties: Mucilage: Mallow tea contains a thick, gel-like substance called mucilage, which coats and soothes the irritated mucous membranes in the throat, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in mallow tea help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the throat, which can contribute to the development of sore throats and other respiratory issues.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Mallow tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and saponins, which can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the throat, providing rapid relief from soreness and pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Malva sylvestris, also known as mallow, teas helps with sore throat because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The tea, made from the leaves and flowers of the mallow plant, contains mucilages that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat. This helps to reduce inflammation, swelling, and pain associated with sore throats. The tea also has antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat infections that cause sore throats. By reducing inflammation and fighting infections, Malva sylvestris teas can provide quick relief from a sore throat.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages are complex carbohydrates found in Malva sylvestris, which help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with sore throats.
Flavonoids (including quercetin): Flavonoids, particularly quercetin, have potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the throat, alleviating symptoms of sore throat.

Saponins: Saponins, a type of triterpene, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacterial and viral infections causing sore throats and reduce inflammation in the throat.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used for their mucilaginous properties that help soothe and calm an irritated throat.
Leaves: Used for their similar mucilaginous properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and discomfort in the throat.
Roots: Used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat infections and reduce swelling in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10-20 grams of dried malva sylvestris flowers or 20-30 grams of fresh leaves and flowers.
Steep the gathered malva sylvestris in 250 milliliters of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the malva sylvestris tea through a fine mesh into a large bowl to remove solids.
Add honey or sugar to taste to the prepared malva sylvestris tea as desired.
Store the remaining malva sylvestris tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Dandelion tea's high antioxidant content helps to reduce inflammation and combat free radicals, which can contribute to sore throat discomfort and speed up recovery.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol and taraxeryl acetate that help to reduce swelling and pain in the throat, providing relief from discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, teas helps with sore throat because its anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and discomfort. remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Taraxacum officinale are commonly used to make teas for sore throat due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Taraxacum officinale are often used to make teas for sore throat due to their antimicrobial and soothing effects.
Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale are sometimes used to make teas for sore throat due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in the morning when they are at their most potent and fragrant.
Gently dry the harvested taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in a warm and dry place for 7 to 10 days.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried taraxacum officinale with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Allow the taraxacum officinale tea to steep for 5 to 10 minutes to release its active ingredients.
Strain the taraxacum officinale tea and serve it hot or iced according to personal preference.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stevia teas helps with sore throat because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of stevia rebaudiana for immediate use or dry them for later brewing.
Combine 10 grams of dried stevia rebaudiana leaves with 500ml of boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean cup to remove solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey and a slice of fresh lemon to taste and adjust sweetness as needed.
Drink the warm herbal stevia rebaudiana tea immediately, allowing the soothing properties to work on your sore throat.

Combine the calendula flowers with 250ml of boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes allowing the flavors and benefits to meld together.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth to remove the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena teas helps with sore throat because of its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. As you sip on the warm tea, these active ingredients help soothe the irritated tissues, reducing pain and discomfort. Expectorant properties: The menthol and eucalyptol present in lemon verbena tea act as natural expectorants, loosening mucus and phlegm in the throat, making it easier to expel and alleviate congestion and coughing.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried verbena officinalis leaves and flowers in amounts of one teaspoon per cup.
Combine the herb with boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or infuser for five minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a large mug or teacup to remove solids.
Add honey or lemon to taste and stir well before serving immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen teas helps with sore throat because of its unique properties. The tea contains a compound called methyl salicylate, which is similar to aspirin in its ability to reduce pain and inflammation. When consumed as a warm tea, it helps to soothe and calm the throat, providing quick relief from discomfort and irritation. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves or use dried form as needed weighing out one teaspoon per cup of boiling water.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to sore throat that are treated with herbal teas are coughs, bronchitis, and laryngitis. Herbal teas such as thyme, eucalyptus, and peppermint help soothe irritated throats and relieve congestion. Ginger tea is also effective in reducing inflammation and calming a scratchy throat. These teas work by providing antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and expectorants that help break down mucus and reduce discomfort.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for sore throat are natural remedies that help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with a sore throat. They work by reducing inflammation, soothing the mucous membranes, and fighting off underlying infections. The best herbal teas for sore throat include Echinacea purpurea, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Thymus vulgaris, Sambucus nigra, Eucalyptus globulus, Ulmus rubra, Althaea officinalis, Achillea millefolium, and Melissa officinalis. If left untreated, a sore throat can lead to difficulty swallowing, loss of appetite, fatigue, and even affect sleep and work productivity, ultimately impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with sore throat is a blend of soothing and antibacterial properties. Combine the anti-inflammatory properties of ginger and turmeric with the antibacterial properties of peppermint and eucalyptus. Add a touch of licorice root for its soothing and anti-inflammatory effects. This combination helps to reduce inflammation, kill bacteria, and ease pain, providing quick relief from a sore throat. You can steep the herbs individually or blend them to create a potent and effective tea.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y.), a traditional demulcent herbal tea, Throat Coat, was found to be effective in providing relief for sore throat. The study, a multicenter, prospective, randomized, double-blinded, placebo-controlled clinical trial, involved 60 patients who were randomly assigned to receive either Throat Coat or a placebo, four to six times daily, for a study period of 2 to 7 days. The results showed that compared to placebo, Throat Coat significantly reduced the intensity of throat pain when swallowing, with a statistically significant improvement of 27.3 in the sum of pain intensity differences (SPID) and a 21.2 increase in total pain relief (TOTPAR), indicating that herbal teas like Throat Coat are good for sore throat.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas helps with sore throat because it contains compounds that reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce swelling and discomfort.

Alkylamides: These compounds have immunomodulatory effects, which help boost the immune system and fight off infections that cause sore throat.
Caffeic acid derivatives: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and protect the throat from further damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are often used to make teas due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as alkylamides and glycosides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: They are commonly used in teas because they contain a range of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help soothe a sore throat.
Flowers: They are typically used in teas for their purported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are thought to aid in the treatment of a sore throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the tea reaches your desired strength and flavor.
Strain the tea into a cup and enjoy immediately while the echinacea purpurea's medicinal properties are still active.

Heat 8 ounces of water to 212 degrees fahrenheit in a teapot or kettle for 5 minutes.
Steep the glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the hot water for 5-7 minutes to allow flavors to release.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids to remove any loose particles completely.
Allow the tea to cool for 2-3 minutes before consumption to prevent burning the tongue slightly.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of dried ulmus rubra bark from a trusted supplier or local foraging expert.

Combine 2 teaspoons of the dried ulmus rubra bark with 1 quart of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
